title: Valerie Brathwaite, Whose Curvaceous and Colorful Sculptures Channeled the Natural World of the Caribbean, Is Dead
summary: Trinidadian-born Caracas-based abstract sculptor Valerie Brathwaite, who drew on what she described as the “overwhelming beauty” of nature to create vibrantly hued organic forms, has died. She was variously reported to have been between eighty-six and eighty-eight. Her death was announced on July 6 by the Museo de Arte Latinoamericano de Buenos Aires (MALBA) in an Instagram post. […]

fullArticle:

Trinidadian-born Caracas-based abstract sculptor Valerie Brathwaite, who drew on what she described as the “overwhelming beauty” of nature to create vibrantly hued organic forms, has died. She was variously reported to have been between eighty-six and eighty-eight. Her death was announced on July 6 by the Museo de Arte Latinoamericano de Buenos Aires (MALBA) in an Instagram post. One of Venezuela’s most celebrated artists of the late twentieth and early twenty-first centuries, Brathwaite transformed ceramic, clay, concrete, fabric, and plaster into sinuous and inviting shapes that reflected the flora, fauna, and landsc...
